observations.summary: The motif of a shattered instrument and a heavy tongue speaking a foretold truth that is only heard as noise parallels Dan's internal struggle with communication and judgment, reflecting his voicelessness and the burden of truth in the narrative.

source_evidence.quote: "When you speak, no man shall understand. Your instrument shall be shattered. Your tongue shall be made a heavy thing, speaking a foretold truth that is only ever heard as noise—the worthless babble of the judged.

observations.target_hint: Dan's loss of voice and the theme of judgment and misunderstood truth throughout the narrative, especially in early chapters describing his condemnation and internal conflict.

target_evidence.quote: "When [PRIMORDIAL_SEVERE_MANDATE] speakest, no man shall understand. [PRIMORDIAL_SEVERE_MANDATE] instrument shall be shattered. [PRIMORDIAL_SEVERE_MANDATE] tongue shall be made a heavy thing, speaking a foretold truth that is only heard as noise—the worthless babble of the judged."
